# `energy/state_distribution_td_loss_pct`

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by `python -m yen_gov indicator-pages`. Do not hand-edit. -->

**Title**: Transmission & Distribution losses (%, by state)  
**One-line**: Energy lost between the point of generation/import into the state grid and the point of metered sale to consumers, as a percentage of total energy input.  
**Source artifact last modified**: 2026-05-16 (auto-generated)  
**Source artifact**: [`datasets/indicators/in/energy/state_distribution_td_loss_pct.json`](../../../../datasets/indicators/in/energy/state_distribution_td_loss_pct.json)

## Definition

Energy lost between the point of generation/import into the state grid and the point of metered sale to consumers, as a percentage of total energy input. T&D losses are the *technical* component — heat in conductors, transformer losses, ageing infrastructure — and exclude commercial losses (theft, billing/collection failure). Compare against ``state_atc_losses_pct`` which adds the commercial component on top: AT&C ≈ T&D + commercial losses.

## Coverage

- **Temporal**: 2009-04..2024-04 (16 periods)
- **Spatial**: India (states + UTs) (36 entities)
- **Rows on disk**: 552

## Methodology vintage

NITI Aayog ICED ``/energy/electricity/distribution/operationalPerformanceStates`` (PFC report-card upstream). Category ``transmission-and-distribution-loss``.

## Notes

Indian central-government targets envision T&D losses below 12% by FY26; the all-India figure has historically been ~17–20%. Big spreads across states reflect grid age, consumer mix, and agricultural pumping share more than utility competence alone.
